title: Massive Global Funding Opportunity: Submit Your Expression of Interest for the GPE Education Out Loud Grant Agent
summary: If your organization works to transform education systems through civil society engagement, transparency, and accountability, this is a career-defining global opportunity you can t afford to miss. The Request for Expression of Interest: GPE Education Out Loud Grant Agent is now officially open. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); What Is Education Out Loud (EOL)? Education Out Loud (EOL) is the largest global fund exclusively dedicated to supporting civil society engagement in education policy, public accountability, and transparency financed by the Global Partnership for Education (GPE) and implemented globally. EOL empowers civil society organizations to influence education planning, strengthen accountability mechanisms, and shape policies to improve learning outcomes for millions, especially the most marginalized. About GPE EOL The Global Partnership for Education (GPE) is the world s largest global fund dedicated to transforming education in low- and middle-income countries. GPE provides strategic finances, policy support, and advocacy leadership to help countries achieve equitable learning outcomes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); As the manager of one of GPE s key advocacy instruments, Education Out Loud supports civil society organizations to: Increase transparency and monitoring of public education Influence national and regional policy reforms Build accountability mechanisms Strengthen data, research, and evidence-generating capacities ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Purpose of the Grant Agent Role GPE is seeking qualified organizations or consortia to serve as the next phase Grant Agent for Education Out Loud. The selected agent will: Oversee and manage the EOL fund portfolio across GPE partner countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Provide fiduciary and operational oversight of grants. Drive strategic support for civil society engagement in education advocacy . Strengthen learning, monitoring, evaluation, and communication across the portfolio. Work closely with GPE, civil society partners, and key stakeholders globally. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This role influences how education advocacy and accountability are supported at scale across regions and political contexts. Who Can Apply? This call is open to: Not-for-profit organizations, international NGOs, multilaterals, civil society organizations Consortia / partnerships of organizations Entities with proven capacity to manage large, complex global grant portfolios Indirect cost recovery is capped (e.g. at 7% per some public posts shared about eligibility). In practice, eligible applicants will have: Strong financial management, transparency, and accountability systems Experience working across diverse regions and contexts Experience supporting civil society and advocacy networks Technical expertise in education policy, civic engagement, and civil sector development ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Capacity to manage complex, multi-stakeholder global grant portfolios (Exact eligibility criteria are detailed in the official EOI docs linked above) What to Submit Your Expression of Interest should demonstrate: Organizational capacity systems, staffing, and financial controls Experience in civil society and advocacy funding Global or multi-regional reach and language capabilities Potential to manage fiduciary compliance, learning, and accountability frameworks Proposed consortium arrangements (if applicable) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Applications usually require: Cover letter / EOI statement Organizational profile and legal status Financial documents and audit reports Team CVs / resumes Proposed approach to managing EOL portfolio (Refer to the official call document for the exact template) Benefits of Becoming the Grant Agent Serving as the Education Out Loud Grant Agent provides: 1. Global leadership platform steward one of the world s largest civil society education funds 2. Influence on education advocacy policy processes worldwide 3. Strengthen institutional reputation in education systems transformation 4. Strategic partnerships with GPE, governments, NGOs, and civil society 5. Opportunity for organizational growth and learning in complex program management ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Start early deadlines are strict. Register for the webinar to clarify expectations. Prepare a comprehensive EOI narrative demonstrating impact, capacity, and accountability . Strengthen your consortium with partners that bring regional depth and technical expertise. Key Deadlines Timeline Milestone Date Call published January 2026 Information Webinar 23 February 2026 (07:00 ET) Register submit questions in advance by 16 February 2026 . EOI Submission Deadline 27 March 2026 Review / Selection Through June December 2026 Expected Start of Next Phase January 2027 Note: Webinar Q A and FAQ documents will be published after the webinar to support applicants. Apply here For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Massive Global Funding Opportunity: Submit Your Expression of Interest for the GPE Education Out Loud Grant Agent appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CAMHRA Masters Scholarships 2026/2027 at SOAS: Full Tuition Funding for Mental Health Anthropology Studies
summary: The Centre for Anthropology and Mental Health Research in Action (CAMHRA) is pleased to announce the CAMHRA Masters Scholarships 2026/2027 at SOAS University of London. These prestigious scholarships cover full tuition fees for eligible students pursuing postgraduate study in the anthropology of mental health. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); With funding available for both UK and overseas applicants, this opportunity supports outstanding candidates committed to advancing research and practice at the intersection of anthropology and mental health. Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 (11:59pm UK local time) About CAMHRA CAMHRA is a UKRI-funded research centre based in the SOAS Department of Anthropology and Sociology. It serves as a globally oriented, locally connected hub for innovative anthropological research, education, and public engagement on mental health. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Working with academic institutions, healthcare providers, policymakers, and service users, CAMHRA contributes anthropological perspectives to global conversations on mental distress, systems of care, and healing practices. Eligible Programmes Scholarships are available for the following programmes beginning in 2026/27: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); MA Medical Anthropology and Mental Health (Full-time: 1 year | Part-time: 2 years) MRes Social Anthropology (Full-time: 1 year | Part-time: 2 years) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The MA programme integrates social, cultural, medical, and psychological anthropology, while the MRes is research-focused and typically undertaken in preparation for PhD study. Scholarship Benefits Full tuition fee coverage for 2026/27 For part-time students, tuition fees covered for 2026/27 and 2027/28 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Approximately three overseas fee places and four home fee places awarded annually One additional international scholarship with a living stipend for applicants from Afghanistan, Iran, or Iraq (subject to annual regional designation) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Please note: Standard scholarships cover tuition fees only and do not include accommodation or living costs. Exceptional Circumstances Stipend For 2026/27, one additional scholarship including a living stipend (in line with UKRI studentships) is available for students who are resident in, or citizens of, Afghanistan, Iran, or Iraq. The stipend in 2025/26 was 22,780 for the academic year (inclusive of London weighting). Eligible applicants must indicate this in their scholarship application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: For MA Medical Anthropology and Mental Health: Hold a minimum 2:1 bachelor s degree in social sciences or humanities; OR Hold a degree in health sciences such as medicine, psychology, nursing, or social work ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); For MRes Social Anthropology: Hold a minimum 2:1 bachelor s or master s degree Applicants without an anthropology background must hold a master s degree in another field All applicants must: Propose a strong research project using ethnographic methods related to mental health ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Submit a separate application to the relevant SOAS master s programme Demonstrate academic merit and research potential Application Process Step 1: Apply to your chosen programme (MA or MRes). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Step 2: Submit your scholarship application via the SOAS admissions portal by 30 April 2026 (11:59pm UK time). Step 3: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an online interview in June 2026. Final offers are expected by July 2026. In the previous cycle, approximately 150 applications were received, making this a highly competitive award. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Important Information for International Students Successful international applicants must apply for a UK Student visa and demonstrate sufficient funds to meet living requirements in London (minimum 13,761 as per UK government guidance). Why Apply? The CAMHRA Masters Scholarships are designed to support individuals who may otherwise be unable to pursue postgraduate study in anthropology due to financial, social, or time constraints. The programme particularly welcomes: Applicants working in mental health services Individuals with lived experience of mental health services Candidates from underrepresented groups in anthropology ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); This scholarship offers an exceptional opportunity to develop ethnographic research expertise, contribute to global mental health debates, and prepare for advanced doctoral study or professional leadership. How to Apply Applying for the CAMHRA Masters Scholarships 2026/27 is a two-step process: Step 1 Apply to your chosen programme: For the MA in Medical Anthropology and Mental Health , submit your application via the Taught Masters applications portal . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); For the MRes in Social Anthropology , apply through the SOAS Doctoral School portal . Ensure you meet the programme-specific eligibility criteria before applying. Step 2 Apply for the CAMHRA Scholarship: Once your programme application is submitted, access the scholarship application through the SOAS admissions portal . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Complete the application by 30 April 2026, 11:59pm UK local time . You will be asked to outline your motivation, proposed research project, and how your studies will make a positive impact in the field of mental health. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an online interview in June 2026. Final scholarship offers will be made by July 2026. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Important: You must submit a programme application to access the scholarship application, but you do not need to have received an offer of admission before the scholarship deadline. For queries, contact scholarships@soas.ac.uk . This process ensures that your scholarship application is considered alongside your programme application, so prepare both carefully to showcase your research potential and commitment to the anthropology of mental health. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); For further information: Programme enquiries: mastersadmissions@soas.ac.uk Centre enquiries: camhra@soas.ac.uk For more scholarships: Click Here The post CAMHRA Masters Scholarships 2026/2027 at SOAS: Full Tuition Funding for Mental Health Anthropology Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Open Call for Knowledge Transfer: €25,000 Grants for Digital Training in Sustainable Food Systems
summary: The STREAMING initiative has officially launched its Open Call for Knowledge Transfer , inviting organizations across Africa and Europe to design and implement innovative digital training programs that strengthen sustainable food trade systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); With 25,000 awarded per project and a total of 11 grants available , this call presents a significant opportunity for institutions committed to advancing food system innovation, women s empowerment, and cross-continental collaboration. At least 60% of the funding will support African Third Parties , with special encouragement for women-led organizations and female innovators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Open Call This Open Call supports the development of interactive, practice-oriented digital courses that bridge the gap between research and real-world application. The goal is to connect researchers with: Farmers Food producers Start-ups Public health actors Entrepreneurs and processors ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Projects must focus on equipping these groups with practical skills to enhance sustainability, innovation, and resilience in food systems. A central priority of the program is women s empowerment , ensuring that women farmers, entrepreneurs, processors, and innovators are actively targeted and supported. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Grant Details Funding Amount: 25,000 per project Total Projects Funded: 11 Geographic Focus: Africa and Europe Funding Allocation: Minimum 60% to African Third Parties Applicants may come from any country , not limited to STREAMING partner nations. Thematic Areas Proposed training programs should address innovations in one or more of the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Traceability in the food supply chain Life cycle assessment Nutritional requirements Reducing micronutrient deficiencies Food waste reduction Sustainable farming systems Food safety Scaling innovations in food supply chains Programs should be practical, interactive, and focused on real-world application. Who Can Apply Eligible Third Parties include: Research institutions and universities NGOs and industry partners Innovation hubs Business schools Vocational colleges Consortia across Africa and Europe ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Consortium applications are welcome, and women-led organizations are strongly encouraged to apply. Application Documents Applicants must carefully review and submit the required documentation included in the Open Call Kit: Official Open Call for Proposals publication notice Knowledge Transfer Call text Proposal template (to be submitted as a PDF) Declaration of Honour (mandatory) Consortium Agreement (mandatory for consortia) Declaration on double funding (signed at submission and project completion) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Applicants are strongly advised to review all materials thoroughly before submission. Deadline Applicants should consult the official Open Call documentation for the final submission deadline and ensure timely submission of all required documents. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Contact Information For administrative questions related to the application process, applicants may contact: Email: STREAMING@bayfor.org Additional resources available: Click Here Appendices: Appendix 1 Open Call for Proposals Appendix 2 Knowledge Transfer Call Document Appendix 3 Proposal Template Appendix 4 Declaration of Honour Absence of Conflict of Interest Appendix 5 Consortium Agreement Appendix 6 Declaration on Double Funding for Recipients This Open Call represents a powerful opportunity to strengthen sustainable food systems, promote innovation, and empower women across Africa and Europe through digital knowledge transfer initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); For more Grant Opportunities: Click Here The post Open Call for Knowledge Transfer: €25,000 Grants for Digital Training in Sustainable Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Consilium Traineeships at the Council of the European Union 2026–2027: Paid EU Internship Opportunities in Brussels
summary: Are you looking for a paid European Union traineeship that offers real experience inside EU institutions? The Consilium Traineeship Programme at the Council of the European Union provides young professionals with the opportunity to work directly with EU policy experts, gain institutional experience, and contribute to high-level decision-making processes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Applications are now open for the upcoming traineeship cycle a highly competitive programme designed for ambitious graduates interested in European affairs, governance, law, policy, and international cooperation. What Are Consilium Traineeships? The Consilium traineeship programme offers first-hand exposure to how the Council of the European Union and its General Secretariat operate. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The name Consilium comes from the Latin word meaning counsel or deliberation , reflecting the Council s role in shaping EU decisions and supporting member states in policy coordination. As a trainee, you will: Gain practical experience within EU institutions Work alongside experienced advisors and policy professionals Contribute to the Council s daily activities Develop professional skills in an international environment Build networks across European institutions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The programme is ideal for graduates interested in EU governance, policy-making, international relations, law, public administration, or related fields. Traineeships are paid and include allowances and other benefits. Around 110 traineeship positions are available each year. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Your traineeship: tasks, grants, allowances Paid Traineeship Benefits The Consilium traineeships are fully paid and include financial support designed to help trainees live and work in Brussels. Key benefits include: Paid monthly grant Additional allowances and benefits Professional training and mentoring Exposure to real EU policy work International multicultural working environment Approximately 110 traineeship positions are offered each year, making it one of the most attractive EU traineeship opportunities for early-career professionals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Current Application Window (2026 Cycle) Applications are currently open for the next traineeship term. Important dates: Application deadline: 19 March 2026 (12:00 Brussels time) Traineeship period: 1 September 2026 31 January 2027 Location: Brussels, Belgium Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid last-minute technical issues. APPLY HERE Positive Action Programme for Trainees with Disabilities The Council reserves up to six traineeships per year for EU nationals with a recognised disability. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This programme ensures inclusive participation by offering: Equal access to EU policy experience Special workplace arrangements when required Supportive and accessible working conditions Participants gain the same high-quality exposure to EU decision-making while benefiting from tailored accommodations. Apply for the positive action programme Positive action programme for trainees with a disability ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Can Apply? Consilium traineeships are open to EU citizens who meet specific eligibility criteria outlined by the Council. The programme targets: Young graduates and early-career professionals Candidates interested in EU affairs and policy Individuals seeking professional experience within EU institutions Applicants should review the official eligibility criteria before applying. Who can apply for a traineeship How to Apply for a Council Traineeship The application process is designed to be straightforward and fully online. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Application steps: Check eligibility requirements Prepare required documents Complete the online application form Submit before the deadline The same procedure applies to both: Main traineeship programme Positive action programme for trainees with disabilities Applications are only open during specific periods each year, so timing is important. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to apply for a traineeship Online Information Session To help candidates better understand the programme, the Council is hosting an online information session. Details: Date: 9 March 2026 Time: 14:00 15:00 (Brussels time) Format: Online presentation + Q A Registration: No prior registration required This session is highly recommended for applicants who want insider guidance on the application process. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); No prior registration needed. Follow our info session about Consilium traineeships Consilium Traineeship Calendar The Council offers traineeships twice per year . February June Traineeship Applications: August September Selection interviews: October December Traineeship period: 1 February 30 June September January Traineeship Applications: February March Selection interviews: April June Traineeship period: 1 September 31 January Understanding the cycle helps candidates plan applications well in advance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Traineeships for Students of National Administration Schools In addition to the general programme, the Council also offers specialised traineeships for students enrolled in national administration schools. Key features: Duration: 2 to 5 months Focus on EU processes and institutional policy Designed to strengthen administrative and governance expertise Frequently Asked Questions Candidates can consult the official FAQ section for detailed guidance on: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Eligibility requirements Application documents Selection process Working conditions Financial support Reviewing the FAQ before applying can help strengthen your application. More questions? Check our FAQ to find the answers to most recurrent questions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Traineeships: frequently asked questions Why Apply for a Consilium Traineeship? A traineeship at the Council of the European Union offers more than work experience it provides direct exposure to the heart of European policy-making. Key reasons to apply: Gain practical EU institutional experience Build an international professional network Strengthen your CV for careers in policy, diplomacy, or development Work in Brussels, one of Europe s key political hubs Learn how EU decisions are shaped in real time ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); For young professionals aiming to build a career in European or international affairs, this programme is a powerful stepping stone. Final Tip for Applicants Strong applications clearly demonstrate: Interest in EU affairs Relevant academic or professional background Motivation to contribute to European policy work Ability to work in multicultural environments Prepare your documents early and submit before the deadline to maximise your chances. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); APPLY HERE Source : European Council The post Consilium Traineeships at the Council of the European Union 2026–2027: Paid EU Internship Opportunities in Brussels appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program 2026: Paid Global Internships for Students & Recent Graduates | Complete Guide
summary: The Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program offers a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in international development, finance, research, policy, and innovation. Through paid internships at IDB, IDB Invest, and IDB Lab , participants work alongside global experts and contribute to projects that improve lives across Latin America, the Caribbean, Asia, and Europe. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you are looking to launch your international career with a respected multilateral organization, this guide explains everything you need to know from eligibility requirements to application tips. Why Choose the IDB Internship Program? The IDB internship program is designed to help students and young professionals: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Gain practical experience in international development Work on real projects with global impact Build professional networks across regions Learn about development finance and policy Experience the organizational culture of the IDB Group Interns work directly with teams at IDB Headquarters in Washington, D.C., or in country offices worldwide, with occasional teleworking flexibility depending on the role. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Types of Internship Opportunities 1. Internships at IDB Headquarters (Washington, D.C.) Interns at headquarters support teams delivering operational and corporate solutions to public and private sector clients. Program Structure Duration: 2 months Work schedule: Full-time (40 hours per week) Format: On-site with some flexibility ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Internship Cycles Winter Internship: January 16 March 15 Summer Internship: June 16 August 15 Applications typically open 3 4 months in advance . Important Note: Applicants may apply for a maximum of three internship positions . 2. Internships at Country Offices (COFs) The IDB has offices across Latin America and the Caribbean, as well as Asia and Europe. These internships are designed to strengthen local development capacity while helping interns build careers in their own regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Features Duration: 2 to 6 months (based on business needs) No fixed cohort timeline Continuous recruitment throughout the year Eligibility Requirement You must be: A citizen or legal resident of the country where the office is located, or Hold a valid work permit and reside in that country. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fields of Study in Demand The IDB welcomes students from diverse academic backgrounds, including: Business and Finance Communications and Marketing Data and Technology Economics and Research Environmental Studies Humanities and Social Sciences Law and Policy Linguistic Services Basic Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the IDB Internship Program, applicants must meet the following criteria: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Citizenship Washington, D.C. internships: Must be a citizen of one of IDB s 48 member countries Country Office internships: Must be a citizen or legal resident of the country where applying IDB Invest roles: No citizenship restrictions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Academic Status Active undergraduate student (minimum second year), or Graduate student with at least 50% of coursework completed Availability Must be available to work full-time (40 hours per week) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Additional Requirements Must not have relatives working at IDB, IDB Invest, or IDB Lab Internship must be your first job within the IDB Group ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Required Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents: Updated Resume (CV) University Enrollment Letter (issued within the last 3 months) Academic Transcripts (official or unofficial; issued within the last 3 months) Note: If you advance in the recruitment process, official transcripts may be required. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); How to Apply for IDB Internships Step 1: Online Application Complete the application form and upload your CV in English or Spanish. A cover letter is only required if specified in the job posting. Pro Tips Read the job description carefully Tailor your CV to match the role Keep your resume concise and results-focused Step 2: Assessment Interview If shortlisted, you may be invited to: Complete a technical assessment Attend a formal panel interview with IDB staff ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Pro Tips Follow instructions carefully during assessments Practice interview responses beforehand Be clear, authentic, and concise in your answers Step 3: Final Decision Recruitment timelines may vary. The IDB provides estimated timelines and applicants can follow up with HR for updates. If you are not selected, you are encouraged to apply again as new positions open regularly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Visa Information for International Applicants For internships at headquarters in Washington, D.C., the IDB supports eligible candidates with G4 Visa processing . Key points: Available to non-U.S. citizens and non-permanent residents Eligible dependents may also receive G4 visas Dependents may apply for U.S. work authorization Employees must live within 50 miles of headquarters Visa approval remains at the discretion of the U.S. Department of State. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); What It s Like to Be an IDB Intern Interns often highlight: Exposure to international development projects Networking with global teams Opportunities to contribute to regional impact Professional growth and career clarity Many interns describe the program as a stepping stone into careers in international organizations, development finance, and policy. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) How long does the application process take? The process may take several weeks or months depending on the role. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Do I need a cover letter? Only if requested in the specific job posting. Does the IDB hire globally? Yes. The IDB recruits for headquarters and country offices worldwide. Are there eligibility restrictions? Yes. Citizenship requirements apply for most positions, except some roles at IDB Invest. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); The IDB Internship Program is a strong opportunity for students and recent graduates who want to build careers in international development, economics, finance, and public policy. With paid placements, global exposure, and meaningful project work, the program provides a solid foundation for future leadership in development. If you are serious about building a global career, prepare your documents early, tailor your CV carefully, and apply strategically. Apply Learn More For full program details and available internship opportunities, visit the official IDB careers platform and explore open positions regularly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(142); }); For a detailed list of available positions and Application, click HERE . Apply Now Don t miss this opportunity to gain hands-on experience in international development. Visit our official website to submit your application before the deadline. For further inquiries or accommodations, contact us at diversity@iadb.org . The post Inter-American Development Bank (IDB) Internship Program 2026: Paid Global Internships for Students Recent Graduates | Complete Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
